observation: K506 converts K505's manual savings logging into set-and-forget auto-hooks across all three agent layers (Bishop/Knight/Pawn) and adds LB Test Frame member-install population-scale telemetry. Six phases: A=Bishop MCP tracker, B=git post-commit hook, C=Pawn paste-back detection, D=Test Frame schema, E=calibration pipeline, F=synapse+report.

Phase A adds an in-process session tracker (_sessionTracker) to librarian-mcp/src/server.ts. The registerTool wrapper increments injection_count and accumulates overhead_tokens_estimate (from response.text.length / 4) on every MCP tool call. run_session_end auto-populates substrate_injection_count and substrate_overhead_tokens from the tracker when caller doesn't supply them. New get_session_telemetry tool exposes tracker state for inspection.

Phase B writes post_commit_savings.py (auto-detects K-session from commit message, estimates tokens from git diff line counts at ~45 chars/line, flags estimated:true). install_git_hooks.py installs it to .git/hooks/post-commit (idempotent, appends). knight_tag_watcher.py polls git tag -l 'v-*-K*' and auto-logs on new tags. All estimates carry multiplier_provisional:true AND estimated:true — honest dual-flag.

Phase C adds detect_and_log_pawn_session MCP tool to server.ts. Scans pasted text against 8 Pawn-layer signatures (model name patterns, session tag regexes, PROMPT_PAWN footers). Extracts token counts from footer metadata if present; otherwise estimates from text char counts (35% input, 25% output ratio). Auto-logs to substrate_savings_log.jsonl with agent=PAWN, estimated:true or false, auto_detected:pawn_paste_back.

Phase D adds 7 savings columns to test_frame_results (K502 table): query_input_tokens, query_output_tokens, substrate_injection_tokens, cold_baseline_estimated_tokens, member_friction_confirmations, savings_estimated, savings_logged_at. All nullable for backward compat. New test_frame_savings_aggregate view exposes aggregate for community-empirical dashboard. TestFrameLanding.tsx Privacy & Telemetry panel explains local-only vs opt-in-share, estimated-vs-measured, right-to-deletion.

Phase E ships calibration_runner.py: reads substrate_savings_log.jsonl, computes per-agent session counts / cost means / multiplier variance, flags empirical_multiplier_note='tautological_until_control_pairs_available' because records were generated using the provisional multiplier (not paired cold/hot). Honest: ships infrastructure, doesn't manufacture false precision. GitHub Action k506-savings-calibration.yml runs monthly cron. Writes immutable calibration_log.jsonl append-only.

K506 introduces a dual-flag discipline for savings records: multiplier_provisional:true (from K505, means cold multiplier is R13-derived not yet calibrated from paired control data) PLUS estimated:true (K506 addition, means token counts were derived from diff size or text length, not measured by vendor API). These are orthogonal — a record can be multiplier_provisional:true + estimated:false (measured tokens, provisional multiplier) or both true (git hook auto-log). Dashboard must display both flags distinctly.

After K506 lands + 30 days of LB Test Frame member adoption, the AAAI paper §11 and Sanders/AOC letters can cite: 'X verified members reporting average +Y pp accuracy lift and Z% cost reduction across N captured queries on the public LB Test Frame; data collection runs continuously and is publicly audit-able at librarian.the2ndsecond.com/community-empirical.' This is the population-scale empirical claim that bridges R13 (formal academic) and public-policy audiences.

The _sessionTracker in server.ts is a module-level singleton that resets on every run_session_end call. This is intentional: it assumes one 'session' = one conversation before calling run_session_end. If run_session_end is called mid-conversation, the tracker resets prematurely. This is an acceptable tradeoff — the alternative (keying by session_id) requires every tool to accept a session_id param which breaks the existing API surface. Document for K-future improvement.

K506 Phase E explicitly flags that current 'empirical multipliers' from calibration_runner.py are tautological: counterfactual_cost = actual_cost × provisional_multiplier (it was computed that way). True calibration requires paired control sessions (cold = no substrate, hot = with substrate) run on the same questions. This data will come from R14/R15 quarterly runs and Test Frame cold-vs-hot member comparisons. The runner outputs control_pairs_available:false and an honest prose note. This is the BRIDLE #41 good-name pattern applied to calibration math.

Phase D TestFrameLanding.tsx Privacy & Telemetry section uses four cards: (1) Logged locally—always, (2) Shared with LB—opt-in only, (3) Estimated vs. measured, (4) Right to deletion. Plus a schema footer listing column names. Plain language, no dark patterns. This satisfies K506 D.4 guardrail: member always knows what's logged and what's shared. The opt-in-share toggle default is OFF.

install_git_hooks.py writes .git/hooks/post-commit if not present, appends if present. Uses MARKER string '# K506 Phase B' to detect prior install (idempotent). Also checks git core.hooksPath and warns if custom hooks path is set (hook may need to be installed there instead). Safe to re-run.

K506 converts the substrate-savings claim from Founder-anecdotal-with-formal-R13-anchor to population-scale-auto-measured. The Founder framing: 'the proof in the LB ship' — empirical proof is baked into the install, not a separate research paper. Members run the architecture and it proves itself in their own data, in their own dashboard, with opt-in-share contributing to the public aggregate. This is Innovation #2268+ (population-scale self-proving architecture).

post_commit_savings.py includes a deduplication guard: before appending, it checks if a record with the same session_id + today's date + estimated:true already exists. Skips if found. This prevents double-logging when Knight uses git commit --amend (which re-fires the post-commit hook). The guard is date-scoped, not commit-hash-scoped, so amended commits on different days still get logged.

TS-001 (K506): Cursor IDE does not expose a public session-ended event API for extensions as of 2026-04. Phase B.1 (Cursor extension approach) was evaluated and found non-viable. Fell back to B.2 (git post-commit hook) as primary Knight auto-hook. The hook is robust (fires on every commit, not session end) which is actually tighter granularity — logs per-commit savings, not per-session. Tag-watcher B.3 covers session-level aggregation.
